summary: The user is setting up a 55-gallon aquarium and is considering adding new fish, specifically guppies, neon tetras, and harlequin rasboras, while also exploring shrimp breeding. They have been keeping a log of water tests and tank maintenance, successfully managing algae growth, and have recently installed an Eheim canister filter to improve water quality and circulation.
facts: The user set up a 55-gallon aquarium about six weeks ago. The user is keeping a mix of neon tetras and harlequin rasboras in their aquarium. The user has been using an algae-preventing supplement to manage algae growth in their aquarium. The user removed algae from their decorations using a magnetic algae scraper. The user installed an Eheim canister filter in their aquarium to improve water circulation and oxygenation. The user is using a combination of mechanical and biological media in their Eheim canister filter, including a coarse foam pad, ceramic rings, and bio balls. The user has added activated carbon to their canister filter to help remove impurities and odors from the water. The user plans to perform maintenance on their canister filter every 4-6 weeks. The user has considered adding a UV sterilizer to their filtration system to control algae growth. The user has been monitoring their phosphate levels, which seem to be within acceptable ranges.
